title: Koelreuteria edulis
taxonomy: Koelreuteria edulis is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The genus Koelreuteria is part of the order Sapindales. Within the family Sapindaceae, Koelreuteria edulis is a member of the tribe Sapindae. The species is native to China and is also known as the Chinese golden rain tree.
uses: Koelreuteria edulis, also known as the golden rain tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the roots and bark of Koelreuteria edulis are used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and antibacterial properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Koelreuteria edulis.
benefits: Koelreuteria edulis is also known as the Chinese tree of heaven, and it has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. Its seeds, leaves, and bark have been found to possess various health benefits. The seeds contain a high amount of protein and oil, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. side_effects: Koelreuteria edulis, also known as the goldenrain tree, is generally considered safe when consumed in moderation. However, consuming large quantities of its edible seeds may cause digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and stomach discomfort in some individuals. The seeds may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, due to their high vitamin K content. People with allergies or sensitivities to plants in the Fabaceae family may also experience adverse reactions when consuming Koelreuteria edulis. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional or registered dietitian before using the goldenrain tree as a food or medicinal source.
intro: Koelreuteria edulis, also known as the goldenrain tree or Chinese tree of heaven, is a deciduous tree native to China and other parts of Asia, prized for its vibrant golden flowers and edible seeds. The seeds of Koelreuteria edulis have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Koelreuteria edulis is often prepared as a tea or dried seed powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat a range of health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
